stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • The Pan-African Heritage Repository (PAHR) is led by archivist Yaa Mensah.
  • Mensah directed a project to build an online repository of manuscripts related to the history of West African literary traditions.
  • PAHR digitized over 8,000 manuscripts, including the journals of West African poets and historians.
  • Building the repository didn’t require manuscripts to be removed from the libraries that housed them.
  • Researchers now have far broader access to these manuscripts.
stem: The student wants to explain an advantage of PAHR’s repository being digital. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Over 8,000 manuscripts related to the history of West African literary traditions are part of the PAHR repository.
Among the manuscripts in the PAHR repository are the journals of West African poets and historians.
PAHR’s director, Yaa Mensah, oversaw the creation of an online repository of manuscripts related to West African literary traditions.
By offering online versions of historical manuscripts, PAHR’s repository provides broader access to these materials without removing them from the libraries that house them.

rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. The sentence explains an advantage of PAHR’s repository being digital, noting that the repository provides broader access to manuscripts since they don’t have to be removed from their host libraries.

Choice A is incorrect. The sentence emphasizes the number of manuscripts; it doesn’t explain an advantage of the repository being digital. Choice B is incorrect. The sentence notes types of manuscripts in the repository; it doesn’t explain an advantage of the repository being digital. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence identifies who oversaw the repository’s creation; it doesn’t explain an advantage of the repository being digital.
